chunk_summary: Referral thresholds for allergy and gastroenterology in India emphasize early specialist intervention to prevent prolonged dietary restrictions and address severe or complex cases, especially in resource-limited settings.
learning_objectives: Understand referral criteria for allergy and gastroenterology in pediatric food allergy management.
Identify clinical signs of IgE-mediated reactions, EoE, and FPIES requiring specialist care.
Recognize the importance of early endoscopy and supervised oral food challenges in diagnosis.
Evaluate the impact of limited access to pediatric endoscopy in India on referral thresholds.
key_facts: Referral to allergy specialists is recommended for suspected anaphylaxis, unclear IgE-mediated reactions, or need for supervised oral food challenges (OFC).
Endoscopy is essential for diagnosing eosinophilic esophagitis (EoE), preventing unnecessary dietary restrictions.
Urgent referral is required for esophageal food impaction, hypotension, airway compromise, or persistent lethargy with dehydration.
In India, lower referral thresholds are advised when staple foods are avoided, risking nutritional deficiencies.
Complex multi-trigger FPIES cases require coordinated challenge planning and specialist care.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Refer to an allergy specialist when there is suspected or recurrent anaphylaxis, a history suggestive of IgE-mediated reactions with unclear trigger, or any scenario where a supervised OFC is needed to confirm diagnosis or expand the diet. (Source: EAACI guidelines on the diagnosis of IgE-mediated food allergy)
document_key_topics: Referral thresholds for allergy and gastroenterology
Diagnosis and management of eosinophilic esophagitis (EoE)
Impact of limited access to pediatric endoscopy in India
